mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching recession, which compresses near-term earnings expectations and triggers risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or t-test p-value exceeds 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which supports corporate revenue growth and margin stability, producing positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative or zero Rank IC with forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, confirming no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in a supply-constrained regime signals pricing power and margin expansion for manufacturers, driving short-term equity outperformance as investors price in resilient corporate profitability.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises while SPX 5d returns remain negative for >50% of observations, refuting the pricing power channel.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation index breaching 235 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises real discount rates and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower when CPIAUCSL > 235 compared to baseline periods, or Rank IC remains positive.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized rate sustains corporate earnings momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as equity markets price in continued economic expansion.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth > 2.5% consistently fails to predict positive 20d SPX returns, with t-test p-value exceeding 0.10 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth and stable unemployment indicate a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending without triggering inflation fears, boosting short-term equity risk appetite and generating positive returns.
failure_condition: Joint payroll and unemployment signal shows no predictive power for 5d returns over a 5-year out-of-sample test, with Rank IC near zero.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.4 million units alongside rising industrial production confirms robust construction and manufacturing activity, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon as capital expenditure accelerates.
failure_condition: Signal fails to generate positive IC when housing starts exceed threshold during high-rate environments, indicating rate sensitivity overrides demand strength.
